title: adopt packaged basicly: migrate overlay to yaml, wire verify checks, refresh install
description: Move off the hand-vendored basicly install: one-command uvx install from pushed main (core force-synced with provenance state), overlay fragments migrated from legacy .fragment.md to .fragment.yaml (project-defaults, python-style, no-user-path-leakage) plus a new overlay-authoring override replacing core catalog-authoring, [[verify.checks]] wired (ruff, ruff-format fast; pytest full), and the new scaffolds (vscode tasks kept as-is, basicly-gates CI workflow). Part of the basicly-zrj.15 acceptance run.
close_reason: Migration shipped as ed0efff and validated end-to-end by the basicly-zrj.15 acceptance: install/upgrade/uninstall round-trips clean, overlay YAML customizations effective and byte-stable across upgrades, verify checks gate commits (fast) and pushes (full), and a full harness loop track (term-m3a) ran classify->build->verify->ship inside this repo.

title: Remediate 2026-07 code & config review (24 findings)
description: Fix all 24 findings from the 2026-07-15 two-track review (Python package + terminal config templates). Includes a keybinding scheme redesign (non-conflicting across WezTerm/tmux/zsh/Claude Code on Windows and Linux) and a cheat-sheet refresh (md + html) as the final step. Finding ids P1-P12 (package) and C1-C12 (configs) are referenced in child descriptions.
close_reason: all 24 findings remediated across 20 commits

title: Design non-conflicting keybinding scheme (WezTerm/tmux/zsh/Claude Code, Win+Linux)
description: Findings C1, C7, C8 (design part). Conflicts today: WezTerm leader == tmux prefix (Ctrl+Space); Ctrl+Shift+Q QuitApplication with NeverPrompt; vim-tmux-navigator steals root-table Ctrl+L (breaks clear and Claude Code Ctrl+L). Deliverable: documented scheme in the epic/plan that keeps tmux prefix reachable from WezTerm, avoids Claude Code keys (Ctrl+O/Ctrl+B/Ctrl+L), and works on Windows and Linux.
close_reason: Scheme decided and recorded in comment; implementation in term-crr.2/.3/.5

comments.author: niksa
comments.text:
DECISION — keybinding scheme (Windows + Linux):
1. tmux keeps prefix Ctrl+Space. It must work in every host (WezTerm, Windows Terminal, VS Code, SSH); Ctrl+A conflicts with readline beginning-of-line, Ctrl+B with Claude Code's background key.
2. WezTerm leader moves Ctrl+Space -> Ctrl+Shift+Space. Same physical location (Shift added), free on Windows and Linux, and unlike Alt+Space it does not collide with the Windows window system menu.
3. Remove Ctrl+Shift+Q (QuitApplication) entirely: with window_close_confirmation=NeverPrompt a mistype kills every session.
4. Drop vim-tmux-navigator root-table C-h/j/k/l: restores Ctrl+L (shell clear AND Claude Code /clear); pane navigation remains prefix+h/j/k/l (tmux) and leader+h/j/k/l (WezTerm).
5. New: ScrollToPrompt(-1)/(1) on Ctrl+Shift+UpArrow/DownArrow in WezTerm (requires OSC 133 from term-crr.5).
6. Protected keys never bound at root tables anywhere: Ctrl+O, Ctrl+B, Ctrl+L, Ctrl+R, Ctrl+C, Ctrl+A/E (readline), Ctrl+Space (tmux prefix).

title: zshrc: set EDITOR=micro when micro is installed
description: Finding C10. micro-settings.json is deployed but EDITOR is unset, so edit-zsh and wezterm open-config fall back to nano. Conditional export in zshrc.
close_reason: ss3 variants bound, EDITOR=micro wired, tpm run guarded

title: tmux: enable focus-events
description: Finding C12. tmux.conf lacks 'set -g focus-events on'; focus reporting never reaches pane programs though WezTerm supports it.
close_reason: osc 7/133 hooks, ScrollToPrompt bindings, allow-passthrough and focus-events enabled

title: configs: resolve WSL guest home via $HOME, not the Windows username
description: Finding P1. deploy_wsl_configs (configs.py:316-333) builds /home/<Windows-profile-name>/...; crashes or misdeploys whenever Windows and WSL usernames differ. Resolve $HOME inside the guest (sh -c).
close_reason: fixed with regression tests (commits ae9889d, 3a72dce)

title: runner: write deployed files with LF newlines on Windows
description: Finding P2. write_text/copy (runner.py:214,221, symlink fallback :243) emit CRLF on Windows; deployed statusline.sh fails in Git Bash with $'\r' errors while setup reports success. Pass newline='\n'.
close_reason: fixed with regression tests (commits ae9889d, 3a72dce)

title: platform: OS-appropriate VS Code settings fallback path
description: Finding P3. get_vscode_settings_path (platform.py:180-189) falls back to ~/.vscode/settings.json which VS Code never reads as user settings; fresh installs silently no-op. Fall back per-OS (AppData/Roaming/Code/User, ~/.config/Code/User).
close_reason: os-appropriate fallback with tests

title: prerequisites: install starship into the WSL guest when run from Windows
description: Finding P4. ensure_starship (prerequisites.py:1671-1700) only handles the Windows binary; guest package list omits starship though deployed zshrc inits it. Flagship WezTerm->WSL flow gets a broken prompt and a permanent report warning.
close_reason: guest install wired with regression test

title: prerequisites: version-check and update user-local tools on re-run
description: Finding P5. ensure_wsl_tools --no-sudo (prerequisites.py:1451-1454) and ensure_node (:1160-1169) are presence-only, freezing tools at first-install versions and violating the re-run-to-update contract. Generalize the lazygit version-compare pattern.
close_reason: opt-in --update flag with regression test; plain re-runs stay presence-checked by design

title: prerequisites: validate GitHub release lookups and handle rate limiting
description: Finding P6. Latest-release lookups (prerequisites.py:985,1058,1082) use curl -s without -f or tag validation; a rate-limited run builds malformed URLs and dies with opaque curl (22). Validate resolved tags like _install_node_binary does; print actionable guidance.
close_reason: shared validated lookup snippet with string-level tests

title: cli/runner: top-level error handler surfacing child stderr; check wsl presence
description: Finding P7. No main() exception wrapper; CalledProcessError hides captured stderr; install_wsl_ubuntu (prerequisites.py:567) runs wsl even when which('wsl') was None (raw FileNotFoundError). Wrap main(), print child stderr and guidance on all exit paths.
close_reason: main() wrapper with tests and functional check

title: document checksum-less downloads (shellcheck, curl|sh installers)
description: Finding P8. shellcheck download (prerequisites.py:1051-1068) lacks both checksum and the deliberate-omission comment its peers carry; rustup/xh/uv/starship curl|sh paths are the integrity floor. Document the policy inline.
close_reason: policy documented inline, matching direnv

title: wezterm: apply keybinding scheme (leader collision, unsafe QuitApplication)
description: Findings C1 + C7. wezterm.lua:318 leader Ctrl+Space shadows tmux prefix; wezterm.lua:328 Ctrl+Shift+Q QuitApplication with window_close_confirmation=NeverPrompt (wezterm.lua:158) kills all windows on a mistype. Apply the scheme from the design bead.
close_reason: leader moved to Ctrl+Shift+Space; QuitApplication binding removed

title: configs: reject/escape shell metacharacters in --wsl-terminal-cwd
description: Finding P9. The value lands inside zsh -lc 'cd "..."' with only Lua escaping (configs.py:24-26, wezterm.lua:15); quotes break every new tab, $() executes at terminal launch. Validate/escape for the shell context too.
close_reason: validation with tests

title: prerequisites: idempotent Windows PATH update preserving REG_EXPAND_SZ
description: Finding P10. _add_to_user_path (prerequisites.py:1703-1715) round-trips expanded PATH (flattens %USERPROFILE% entries to REG_SZ) and the forward-slash -notlike check misses existing entries, duplicating PATH on each re-run.
close_reason: registry-safe idempotent update, parse-checked against real powershell

title: cli: host report probes ~/.local/bin like the WSL report does
description: Finding P11. _print_host_report (cli.py:270-305) checks PATH only; after a --no-sudo install on native Linux the report marks freshly installed tools missing. Probe ~/.local/bin explicitly like _wsl_command_present.
close_reason: host report probes ~/.local/bin

title: tests: cover script builders, deploy_wsl_configs, report probes, runner matrix
description: Finding P12. Zero coverage for the riskiest code: sh/PowerShell script builders, deploy_wsl_configs, print_setup_report probes, platform.py WSL parsing, Runner.run check/dry_run_safe matrix, ConsoleReporter.confirm EOF. Add string-level and unit tests alongside the fixes.
close_reason: coverage added across the epic plus dedicated probe/script batch

title: cheat sheet: update md and regenerate html for the new bindings and features
description: User-requested final step. Reflect the new keybinding scheme, clipboard behavior, ScrollToPrompt navigation, and any changed defaults in terminal-cheat-sheet.md, then regenerate terminal-cheat-sheet.html via .scripts/render-cheat-sheet.py.
close_reason: md updated and html regenerated

title: tmux: apply keybinding scheme (prefix reachability, navigator Ctrl+L clash)
description: Findings C1 + C8. tmux.conf:8 prefix Ctrl+Space unreachable under WezTerm leader; tmux.conf:69 vim-tmux-navigator root-table C-h/j/k/l breaks Ctrl+L clear and Claude Code. Apply the scheme from the design bead.
close_reason: navigator dropped, hjkl navigation added, prefix documented

title: tmux: copy-mode selections reach the system clipboard via OSC 52
description: Finding C2. tmux.conf:51-53 copy-selection lands only in tmux internal buffer; nothing crosses WSL to the Windows clipboard. Fix: set-clipboard on, terminal-features ',xterm*:clipboard', copy-selection-and-cancel.
close_reason: osc 52 clipboard wired

title: shell integration: OSC 7/133 in zshrc, ScrollToPrompt in wezterm, allow-passthrough in tmux
description: Finding C3. No OSC 7/133 emitted, so wezterm cwd-based tab titles are empty for WSL panes and jump-to-prompt is impossible in huge agent output. Add precmd/preexec integration to zshrc, ScrollToPrompt bindings to wezterm.lua, allow-passthrough on to tmux.conf.
close_reason: osc 7/133 hooks, ScrollToPrompt bindings, allow-passthrough and focus-events enabled

title: zshrc: bind SS3 application-mode variants for arrows/Home/End
description: Finding C4. zshrc:35-39 binds CSI sequences only; under smkx the keys send SS3 so history-search and Home/End never fire in WezTerm. Bind both variants or use terminfo caps.
close_reason: ss3 variants bound, EDITOR=micro wired, tpm run guarded

title: statusline.sh: LC_NUMERIC=C and validate resets_at before arithmetic
description: Findings C5 + C11. printf %.2f honors LC_NUMERIC (comma-decimal locales corrupt cost math, statusline.sh:179-185); resets_at feeds $(( )) unvalidated (statusline.sh:128-130). Export LC_NUMERIC=C; guard non-epoch values; add a fixture-driven test for the jq contract.
close_reason: locale-proofed with fixture tests; starship timeout raised

title: tmux: guard tpm run (or install tpm during deploy)
description: Finding C6. tmux.conf:77 unguarded run of ~/.tmux/plugins/tpm/tpm which the setup never installs: startup error on fresh machines, plugins silently absent. if-shell guard with install hint, or clone tpm in configs.py.
close_reason: ss3 variants bound, EDITOR=micro wired, tpm run guarded

title: starship: stop git_metrics timeout noise on large repos
description: Finding C9. starship.toml:9 command_timeout=500 with git_metrics enabled produces per-prompt timeout warnings on big repos. Raise timeout or disable git_metrics.
close_reason: locale-proofed with fixture tests; starship timeout raised

title: trim overlay so generated agent files fit the 8000 cap
description:
The overlay migration (term-7xk) pushed the generated always-on files over basicly's 8000-char soft cap: .claude/CLAUDE.md is 8005 and .github/copilot-instructions.md 8102 (build prints both warnings). Per the cap discipline the remedy is trimming overlay prose, not core content: the overlay-authoring body carries a long inline uvx command that duplicates the VS Code task, and project-defaults has a redundant bullet.

## Acceptance Criteria

- basicly build emits no size warnings; both files are at or under 8000 chars.
- Only overlay fragment bodies change (no core edits, no generated-file hand-edits).
- Regenerated projections still contain the Overlay Authoring and Project Defaults sections.
close_reason: shipped by the harness loop

title: cut v0.4.0 release
description: Release the 2026-07 review remediation (epic term-crr: keybinding scheme, osc 7/133 integration, clipboard, statusline and installer fixes, --update flag) plus the packaged basicly adoption. Version bump, changelog, tag v0.4.0, workflow verification.
